definitions.definition: A collection of data stored on a storage device, such as a hard disk or solid state drive.
A set of operations performed on files, including creating, reading, writing, and deleting files.
A sequence of bytes flowing through a program, used for input/output operations.
A stream that reads or writes data to a file.
A stream that reads data from a source, such as the keyboard or a file.
A stream that writes data to a destination, such as the screen or a file.
A file that can be accessed at any location, using a file pointer.
A file that is accessed in a linear sequence, one record at a time.
A pointer that keeps track of the current location in a file.
A region of memory used to temporarily store data being read or written to a file.
The way a file can be opened, such as read-only, write-only, or read-write.
The actions performed on a file, including create, read, write, delete, and close.
Information about the current state of a file, including its existence and accessibility.
The name given to a file, used to identify it.
The characters that follow the last dot in a file name, indicating the file type.
A location that stores files and subdirectories, similar to a folder.
A sequence of directories and files that identifies the location of a file.
